title: linum usitatissimum var. angustifolium
intro: Linum usitatissimum var. angustifolium, also known as narrow-leaved flax or linseed, is a plant species in the family Linaceae, characterized by its narrow leaves and tall stature. The seeds of this plant have been used for centuries due to their rich content of omega-3 fatty acids and fiber, exhibiting medicinal properties that can help to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health. The health benefits associated with consuming linseed include improved digestion, reduced blood sugar levels, and enhanced immune function, making it a valuable addition to a balanced diet. Linseed is commonly used in herbal preparations such as oil capsules, tea, and flour, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal and health benefits.
